code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about an artist into a single fluent sentence.
    The function builds a simple natural‑language description by mapping known
    predicates to short phrase templates and concatenating the results.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a phrase for a single triple
    def phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
        p = t.predicate
        o = t.object
        # Basic templates for common predicates
        templates = {
            "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {o}",
            "birthYear": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
            "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
            "deathDate": lambda o: f"died on {o}",
            "deathPlace": lambda o: f"died in {o}",
            "origin": lambda o: f"originates from {o}",
            "instrument": lambda o: f"plays the {o}",
            "genre": lambda o: f"belongs to the {o} genre",
            "background": lambda o: f"has a background as {o}",
            "occupation": lambda o: f"is a {o}",
            "recordLabel": lambda o: f"is signed to {o}",
            "nationality": lambda o: f"is {o}",
            "activeYearsStartYear": lambda o: f"started their career in {o}",
            "activeYearsEndYear": lambda o: f"ended their career in {o}",
            "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": lambda o: f"is associated with {o}",
            "bandMember": lambda o: f"has band member {o}",
            "parentCompany": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
            "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
            "capital": lambda o: f"has capital {o}",
            "currency": lambda o: f"uses the {o} as currency",
            "demonym": lambda o: f"its people are called {o}",
            "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o}",
            "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"has official language {o}",
            "populationDensity": lambda o: f"has a population density of {o}",
            "areaTotal": lambda o: f"covers an area of {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is {o} meters above sea level",
            "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal codes {o}",
            "foundingDate": lambda o: f"was founded on {o}",
            "meaning": lambda o: f"means {o}",
            "longName": lambda o: f"is officially named {o}",
            "anthem": lambda o: f"has anthem {o}",
            "currency": lambda o: f"uses the {o} as currency",
            "ethnicGroup": lambda o: f"has ethnic group {o}",
            "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
            "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"leadership title is {o}",
            "governingBody": lambda o: f"is governed by {o}",
            "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
            "distributingCompany": lambda o: f"is distributed by {o}",
            "training": lambda o: f"trained at {o}",
            "recordLabel": lambda o: f"is signed to {o}",
            "alternativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        }
        if p in templates:
            return templates[p](o)
        # fallback – simple “predicate object” phrasing
        return f"{p} {o}"

    subject = triples[0].subject
    # Build phrases, avoid repeating the subject for each triple
    parts = [phrase(t) for t in triples if t.subject == subject]

    # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last element for readability
    if not parts:
        return ""
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence_body = parts[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + f", and {parts[-1]}"

    # Capitalize first character
    sentence = f"{subject} {sentence_body}."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
